label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know which statement is true based on the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to read the passage carefully again. The main points are about the British government reviewing the lottery system for school allocations because over 20% of children can't get into their first-choice schools. The article mentions that in some areas, up to a fifth (which is 20%) face disappointment. Looking at option B: "Over one in five children fail to get into their first-choice schools in parts of Britain." The passage says "more than 20 percent of children are failing to get into their first-choice schools in parts of the country." So "over one in five" is the same as more than 20%, which matches. So B might be correct. Option A: The lottery system is certainly a good way. The passage mentions the government is reviewing it, so they're not sure it's good. Also, there's a mention that in Brighton and Hove, some kids didn't get any of their choices. So the lottery isn't perfect. So A is too strong, the passage doesn't say it's certainly good. Option C: The lottery is welcomed by parents in 25 authorities. The passage says lotteries are used by a small number of schools in 25 local authorities at the government's suggestion. But it doesn't say parents
